title: Nice People and Free Pizza with Agape San Diego
summary: A casual lunchtime hangout in Price Center with free pizza and an easy way to meet people.
description: Join friends and meet new people every Tuesday over free pizza in Price Center East. This is a low-pressure drop-in gathering that is especially good for students who want something social without needing a big plan ahead of time.
whyRecommended: Free food and a familiar central-campus location make this one of the easiest ways to ease into meeting people.

title: Lavender Sachets @ The Zone
summary: A simple creative break between classes with supplies provided in The Zone at Price Center.
description: Stop by The Zone to make your own lavender sachet and take a breather between classes. All supplies are provided for UC San Diego students with a valid student ID, so it works well as an easy campus activity with no prep required.
whyRecommended: It is easy to squeeze in between classes and gives new students a reason to learn where The Zone is.

title: Out and Proud Series Event: Lunch and Craft
summary: A bring-your-own-lunch community craft hour in Geisel with strong campus community energy.
description: Join the UC San Diego LGBT Resource Center and Pride Staff Association for a community lunch-and-craft hour during Out and Proud 2026. It is a welcoming space for reflection, conversation, and making connections in a recognizable campus location.
whyRecommended: A defined activity and a well-known library location make this easier to approach than a totally open social event.

title: WC & RRC Latine Student Org Social Mixer
summary: A guided social mixer for meeting peers involved in Latine student organizations.
description: The Women's Center and Raza Resource Centro are hosting an afternoon mixer with guided conversations about gender, culture, identity, and leadership. Students can meet campus organizations, connect with peers, and build community in a more structured environment.
whyRecommended: The guided format helps take the pressure out of networking and makes it easier to leave with real campus connections.

title: Care Fair
summary: A campus resource fair focused on gender-affirming care, support services, and practical help.
description: Join STRIDE for the Spring Gender-Affirming Care Fair to learn about campus resources and support options. Food is provided, and the setting is designed for students who want concrete help and a clearer picture of where to go for care on campus.
location: Price Center, Cross-Cultural Center / Comunidad Room
whyRecommended: This is useful for students who want practical support and a clear introduction to real campus resources.

title: Cafecito Hour
summary: A welcoming community hour with coffee, conversation, and support in Pepper Canyon Hall.
description: Cafecito Hour brings students together to talk about well-being, academic success, and Latinx experiences in a community-centered space. Coffee and light snacks are provided, and bilingual conversation is welcome.
location: RAZA Resource Center (RRC), Pepper Canyon Hall Room 264
whyRecommended: It combines an approachable room-sized setting with a campus support space that transfer students may not know yet.

title: Summer Budgeting and Finance Tips Workshop @ The Zone
summary: A practical workshop on planning for summer housing, travel, jobs, and changing expenses.
description: Join a University Credit Union advisor at The Zone to learn how to prepare for summer costs, compare job options, and build a short-term budget. It is a focused session for students trying to make smart money choices before the quarter ends.
whyRecommended: This is useful for students balancing internships, housing changes, and summer costs for the first time.

title: Mindfulness Studio
summary: A guided mindfulness break in Geisel focused on breathwork, grounding, and resetting during the quarter.
description: Step into the RISE Mindfulness Studio for a calming workshop built around visualizations, breathwork, and compassion-based meditation. It is a productive low-pressure break in a recognizable library setting.
whyRecommended: It is a strong fit for students who want a gentle first step into campus wellness spaces without a big crowd.

title: Science Communication Workshop and Panel with Industry Experts
summary: A career-focused workshop on translating STEM ideas into compelling stories and professional opportunities.
description: Hosted by the Saltman Quarterly and the School of Biological Sciences Career and Professional Development Program, this session explores science communication skills and related career paths. Students can hear from industry experts and build practical communication skills that stand out across fields.
whyRecommended: It is a good way to explore a career-adjacent event even if you are still figuring out how your major connects to real jobs.

title: Yoga at the Library
summary: A free indoor Vinyasa session in Geisel that helps students learn one of campus' most central spaces.
description: Join the Library and Recreation for indoor Vinyasa Flow in the Geisel Meeting Room. Weekly registration is required, and students should bring a yoga mat and props if possible.
whyRecommended: It pairs a familiar campus landmark with a low-pressure wellness activity that is easy to understand and easy to find.

title: Transfer Hacks Showcase
summary: A transfer-centered showcase of student projects, feedback, and networking in Jacobs Hall.
description: The UC San Diego community is invited to learn about student-led projects from TransferHacks participants and see the winning project announced. Students can mingle, learn, and enjoy light refreshments while getting a feel for the engineering and innovation community on campus.
whyRecommended: It is directly relevant to transfer students and gives a clear reason to visit a major academic building with an easy social script.

title: Stress Better: Skills for Managing Stress
summary: A quick workshop that mixes a simple craft with practical strategies for handling stress and anxiety.
description: Join Student Health and Well-Being at the Craft Center to learn usable stress-management strategies while making a small craft. The session is designed to help students discover concrete techniques for dealing with stress in a more approachable format.
location: Craft Center, Sixth College, Mosaic Second Floor
whyRecommended: This is an easy entry point for students who want support but feel more comfortable showing up for an activity with a clear structure.

title: Black Connections
summary: A welcoming wellness space centered on connection, self-care, and academic success for Black students and allies.
description: Black Connections offers a welcoming environment for discussing Black health, wellness, and academic success. Students can join self-care activities, collective problem solving, and supportive conversation in the Black Resource Center.
whyRecommended: This gives students a clear path into one of UCSD's community resource spaces with a supportive, conversation-based format.

title: Water Beads @ The Zone
summary: A quick hands-on break in Price Center with a low-pressure creative activity and a familiar student space.
description: Come to The Zone and make your own water bead creation during a flexible drop-in window. Materials are provided for active UC San Diego students with a valid student ID, making it an easy between-classes event in a central campus location.
whyRecommended: It is a simple way to get comfortable with Price Center and The Zone without needing to commit to a formal meeting.

title: Pitch Perfect Movie Series @ PC
summary: A free Price Center movie night with student energy, a clear meetup point, and an easy social script.
description: University Centers is hosting a free student movie night for Pitch Perfect at Price Center Theater. Doors open at 6:30 p.m., the screening starts at 7 p.m., and UC San Diego students can attend free with ID. Swag is available for superfans.
location: Price Center, Price Center Theater, PC West Plaza
organizer: University Centers (Price Center and Student Center)
whyRecommended: Movie nights are easy to understand, easy to find, and work well for students who want a social outing without a lot of pressure.

title: Climate Cafe - Native Seed Balls w/ CAPS @ The Zone
summary: A student-friendly conversation on climate anxiety with refreshments and a simple hands-on activity in The Zone.
description: Stop by The Zone for Climate Cafe to make native seed balls, talk about climate justice, and learn practical tools for handling climate anxiety with CAPS. Catered pastries and refreshments are included, and participants are entered to win a gift card.
whyRecommended: It combines a clear activity, food, and a familiar campus location with a topic many students care about but may not know how to approach.

title: Showcase QI
summary: A self-guided visit through Qualcomm Institute projects, research, and interactive campus tech spaces.
description: Visit Showcase QI on the first floor of Atkinson Hall to explore research, education, and shared facilities from the Qualcomm Institute. It works well as a flexible stop-in event for students who want to get more familiar with innovation spaces on campus.
whyRecommended: It is an easy way to explore one of UCSD's major research spaces without needing to show up knowing anyone.

title: STI Awareness Week: Reduced & No Cost STI Testing
summary: A practical Student Health Services event offering reduced-cost walk-in STI testing during awareness week.
description: Student Health and Well-Being is offering reduced and no-cost STI testing during STI Awareness Week, including a free testing day on April 15. This is a practical campus wellness event with clear next steps for students who want direct support.
whyRecommended: It helps students learn where Student Health Services is while also connecting them to a real campus resource they may need later.

title: ERC Health & Wellness Paw-ffice Hours
summary: A casual Roosevelt College wellness event with dogs, donuts, and campus support teams on ERC Green.
description: Join ERC student affairs, CAPS, CARE at SARC, Outback Adventures, and Academic Advising for a wellness-focused drop-in on ERC Green. The mix of dogs, donuts, and student support makes this an approachable community event for newer students.
whyRecommended: Outdoor drop-in events with a clear setup are one of the easiest ways for transfer students to check out a new part of campus.

title: ERC Weekly Paw-ffice Hour with Will Songer and Louie
summary: A weekly Roosevelt College drop-in with a student support staff member and an easy reason to stop by ERC Green.
description: Stop by ERC Green to meet Will Songer and Louie during this weekly Paw-ffice Hour. Students can ask questions about student life and academics or simply say hello in a relaxed outdoor setting.
whyRecommended: It gives newer students a low-stakes reason to explore ERC and talk with someone whose job is to help students get oriented.
